text: The surgeon holds up the stay suture and the knot of the upper suture, demonstrating small suture bites during the anastomosis. The inner portion of the anastomosis shows a whitish discoloration of the mucosa, prompting the surgeon to cut the suture.

The surgeon holds up the stay suture while pulling down on the knot of the upper suture, demonstrating small suture bites in the tissue. The inner portion of the anastomosis shows a whitish discoloration, prompting the surgeon to cut the suture out.
The video reveals a large defect in the posterior wall of the staple line, highlighting a surgical error related to the misplacement of a stay suture during the closure process.
The surgeon demonstrates the placement of a U-stitch on the posterior wall of the anastomosis, using a traction suture to expose the critical part of the staple line for closure.

The surgeon demonstrates the placement of a U-stitch on the back wall of the anastomosis, going from out to in and then in to out, which exposes the critical part of the anastomosis. A traction suture is applied to the back wall to pull it out for better visualization.
